title: cyathea pygmaea
intro: Cyathea pygmaea, also known as the Dwarf Tree Fern, is a species of small to medium-sized tree fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyathea pygmaea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Cyathea pygmaea can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
